articles.headline: Offshore filings reveal how shell companies moved public contract money across three continents
articles.excerpt: A cross-border investigation traces a network of shell entities used to obscure ownership of firms awarded state infrastructure contracts.

articles.headline: Internal cables show aid agencies were warned of supply route collapse weeks before crossings closed
articles.excerpt: Documents obtained by Dropsite show repeated internal warnings went unheeded by coordinating bodies.

articles.headline: How a little-known state fund quietly backstopped a private insurer's bad bets
articles.excerpt: Regulators allowed the fund's exposure to grow far past its own risk limits, records show.

articles.headline: A popular license plate reader company left a database of driver locations exposed for months
articles.excerpt: Researchers found the misconfigured server indexed by a search engine before it was locked down.

articles.headline: Regional talks stall as negotiators clash over terms of proposed border monitoring mission
articles.excerpt: Delegations left the third round of talks without a joint statement for the first time this year.

articles.headline: Nursing home chain kept collecting Medicare payments after inspectors flagged repeat violations
articles.excerpt: State inspection records show citations were issued but rarely resulted in funding holds.

articles.headline: Leaked customs data links sanctioned shipping fleet to routine ports of call in four countries
articles.excerpt: Trade records suggest enforcement gaps let vessels continue operating under new registrations.

articles.headline: Procurement records show contractor billed for equipment never delivered to field hospitals
articles.excerpt: Invoices reviewed by Dropsite do not match delivery logs kept by on-site logistics staff.

articles.headline: Inside the marketplace where data brokers sell location histories with no oversight
articles.excerpt: A review of listings shows granular movement data changing hands for a few hundred dollars.

articles.headline: Shipping costs climb as rerouted vessels avoid contested strait for third straight month
articles.excerpt: Insurers have raised premiums on the route, adding pressure to already stretched freight budgets.

articles.headline: Whistleblower records detail how safety complaints were routed away from federal reviewers
articles.excerpt: Internal routing logs show a pattern of complaints closed without reaching inspection staff.

articles.headline: Real estate registry gaps let sanctioned individuals hold property through family members
articles.excerpt: Public records in three jurisdictions show no requirement to disclose beneficial ownership.
